title: Collections, Taxonomy, Ordering, Navigation, Feeds, and Sitemaps · Runtime Lens: Taxonomy Identity
summary: Navigation depends on filesystem order, tags differ by case, drafts leak into feeds, pagination duplicates pages, orphan content is invisible, and sitemap URLs disagree with canonical routes. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the taxonomy identity increment in reconciled accessible site graph and derived publications. Learners progress through pr…
objectives: Normalize tag and category identity separately from labels, aliases, hierarchy, route, and language.
Select published documents by typed metadata, language, route, taxonomy, and time under deterministic query and ordering rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Predict Taxonomy Identity · Theory Taxonomy Identity
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory publishing team handles case 7239 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. A defensible solution applies Taxonomy Identity, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reconciled accessible site graph and derived publications.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-navigation-taxonomy-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-navigation-taxonomy-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_navigation_taxonomy_2(published: int = 13, navigable: int = 13, taxonomy_terms: int = 5, feed_entries: int = 13, sitemap_urls: int = 13) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if published < 1 or navigable < 0:
        return False, navigable, "publication-count"
    if navigable != published:
        return False, navigable, "orphan-route"
    if taxonomy_terms < 1:
        return False, taxonomy_terms, "taxonomy-shape"
    if feed_entries != published or sitemap_urls != published:
        return False, feed_entries, "derived-publication-drift"
    assert feed_entries == sitemap_urls == published
    return True, navigable, "reconciled-site-graph"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-taxonomy-identity-oojdaq
# Competency: ssg-taxonomy-identity.
# Case 1375: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions.
# Changed case: replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG.
def evidence_ssg_taxonomy_identity_oojdaq(published: int = 13, navigable: int = 13, taxonomy_terms: int = 5, feed_entries: int = 13, sitemap_urls: int = 13) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_oojdaq = "oojdaq-qadjoo-1375"
    assert evidence_variant_oojdaq
    evidence_axes_oojdaq = (
        "readeroojdaq",
        "source1375",
        "parseqadjoo",
        "routeoojdaq1375",
        "artifact1375oojdaq",
        "recoveryqadjoo1375",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_oojdaq)) == 6
    if published < 1 or navigable < 0:
        return False, navigable, "publication-count"
    if navigable != published:
        return False, navigable, "orphan-route"
    if taxonomy_terms < 1:
        return False, taxonomy_terms, "taxonomy-shape"
    if feed_entries != published or sitemap_urls != published:
        return False, feed_entries, "derived-publication-drift"
    assert feed_entries == sitemap_urls == published
    return True, navigable, "reconciled-site-graph"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Taxonomy Identity: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 5429 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile navigation, taxonomy, feeds, and sitemaps to one published route manifest; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Normalize tag and category identity separately from labels, aliases, hierarchy, route, and language.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Taxonomy Identity model · Theory Taxonomy Identity.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Taxonomy Identity model · Theory Taxonomy Identity, keep Taxonomy Identity connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Normalize tag and category identity separately from labels, aliases, hierarchy, route, and language.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Taxonomy Identity model · Theory Taxonomy Identity, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Taxonomy Identity precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Taxonomy Identity model · Theory Taxonomy Identity, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 5429 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile navigation, taxonomy, feeds, and sitemaps to one published route manifest; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Normalize tag and category identity separately from labels, aliases, hierarchy, route, and language.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Taxonomy Identity model · Theory Taxonomy Identity, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Two tag labels that look the same are automatically one taxonomy term.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-taxonomy-identity-oojdaq.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-taxonomy-identity-oojdaq(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_taxonomy_identity_oojdaq\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?published < 1 or navigable < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?navigable != published)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?taxonomy_terms < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?feed_entries != published or sitemap_urls != published)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
